title: What is LV2? The Chicago parking zone that tows your car in minutes.
excerpt: LV2 changed in 2023. It now applies to all Cubs games, not just night games. Here's exactly what the zone covers, what it costs, and how to get a permit.

title: Chicago City Sticker FAQ: What Every Driver in the City Needs to Know
excerpt: Every Chicago resident who owns a vehicle must buy a city sticker from the City Clerk. It's also the first step before getting a resident parking permit for zones like LV2.
label: Rules

title: You Can Apply for Your LV2 Permit Online and Get It Mailed to You
excerpt: If your address is in the 47th Ward portion of the LV2 zone, Mat Martin's office has an online form. Apply, and the permit arrives in the mail. No office visit required.
label: Permits

title: LV2 Guest Pass for Cubs Games: How to Get One for Your Visitors
excerpt: If friends or family are parking at your address on a game day, a standard parking permit won't help them. The 47th Ward issues special LV2 Cubs Game guest passes. Here's how it works.
label: Permits

title: How to Contest a Chicago Parking Ticket (Including LV2 Tickets)
excerpt: You can fight a Chicago parking ticket online, by mail, or in person. LV2 tickets have specific defenses that are worth knowing before you pay.

title: The History of LV2 Parking Near Wrigley Field
